title: 10 best herbal creams for gas in stomach

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with gas in stomach because it contains menthol, a natural relaxant that soothes the muscles in the digestive tract. When we eat certain foods or drinks that are difficult to digest, they can cause bloating and discomfort in the stomach. The menthol in peppermint creams helps to reduce inflammation and spasms in the intestines, allowing for smoother digestion and elimination of gas and bloating. This natural remedy is especially helpful in alleviating symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to bloating and discomfort. This relief from muscle tension allows gases to pass more easily through the digestive system.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in peppermint creams help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can exacerbate symptoms of gas and bloating. By neutralizing free radicals, antioxidants promote a healthier gut environment and alleviate discomfort.

Carminative properties: Peppermint's carminative properties help to eliminate trapped gases from the digestive system by relaxing the muscles and promoting the release of carbon dioxide and other gases. This reduces feelings of bloating and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol and other volatile oils, peppermint roots have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them effective in alleviating symptoms of stomach gas.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from stomach gas and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Steam distill the washed peppermint leaves using a still or steam extractor for 4 hours.
Combine the extracted essential oil with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 10ml:50ml and mix well.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 30 minutes to melt the coconut oil and emulsify the ingredients.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and let it cool completely before refrigerating or freezing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain fennel seeds, a natural anti-inflammatory that relaxes the muscles in the digestive tract. This relaxation reduces bloating and cramps associated with gas, allowing for easier digestion and elimination of trapped air. Additionally, the cream's soothing properties calm irritated mucous membranes, further reducing inflammation and discomfort. As a result, fennel creams provide fast and effective relief from stomach gas and discomfort, promoting a sense of digestive well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel cream's carminative property helps to release trapped gases from the digestive system, reducing bloating and discomfort by relieving flatulence and promoting smooth digestion.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel's anti-inflammatory property soothes and calms the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, which can become irritated and inflamed due to gas and indigestion, thereby reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel cream's digestive stimulant property helps to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of indigestion and bloating by increasing the production of digestive enzymes and promoting the movement of food through the digestive system.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, including flatulence and bloating, due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are often used to make teas or infusions that can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, gas, and bloating by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves from your garden or store dried ones for later use measuring 20 grams of dried leaves per 100 milliliters of carrier oil.
Combine the harvested foeniculum vulgare leaves with a carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil mixing well to ensure even infusion.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heatproof glass bowl set over boiling water for 20 minutes steeping the herbal extract into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a clean glass jar for storage.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax or other emollients such as shea butter and coconut oil melting them together at 60 degrees celsius before pouring into sterilized jars for cream formulation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with gas in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive system. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation and spasms in the gut, allowing for smoother digestion and reducing the occurrence of gas and bloating. Additionally, ginger has been shown to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms associated with indigestion and nausea, providing relief from uncomfortable stomach issues.
remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes helps break down food more efficiently, reducing gas and bloating by promoting proper digestion. This is because it activates the enzyme pepsin, which breaks down proteins into smaller molecules.

Antispasmodic Agent: Ginger's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the stomach, intestines, and digestive tract, reducing spasms that can contribute to gas and bloating. This relaxation helps move food through the digestive system more smoothly.

Inflammation Reducer: Ginger has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which help reduce inflammation in the gut and alleviate symptoms of IBS (Irritable Bowel Syndrome), including gas and bloating. By reducing inflammation, these compounds promote a healthy gut environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in natural compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve stomach discomfort and reduce gas.

Rhyzomes: The thick, edible part of the ginger plant is rich in vitamins A and C, as well as minerals like potassium, calcium, and iron, making it an effective ingredient for soothing digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ginger root by digging up mature plants from your garden or purchasing from a reputable supplier.
Dry the fresh ginger root slices in a single layer at room temperature for 7-10 days or until they reach a brittle texture.
Grind the dried ginger root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to yield about 1 cup of powdered ginger.
Mix the powdered ginger with coconut oil and beeswax in a heat-proof mixing bowl, warming it gently over low heat for about 15 minutes until fully incorporated.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for at least 2 hours before whipping into a smooth and creamy consistency using an electric mixer or whisk.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with gas in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The citral compound present in lemongrass has a gentle relaxing effect on the muscles, allowing for easier digestion and reducing bloating. Additionally, lemongrass's antimicrobial properties help to break down trapped air and gas in the stomach, promoting regular bowel movements and alleviating discomfort associated with indigestion and flatulence.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Lemongrass has carminative properties, which help to relieve bloating and flatulence by reducing the formation of gas in the digestive tract.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic compounds in lemongrass cream can help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, reducing spasms that can contribute to gas and discomfort.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help to soothe and protect the digestive tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate gas and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemongrass are used to create calming and anti-inflammatory properties in creams, which help soothe stomach discomfort caused by gas.
Rhyzomes: Lemongrass rhyzomes contain natural antacids that can neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from bloating and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves weighing approximately 200 grams and rinse thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the harvested leaves with distilled water at a ratio of 1:4 and heat the mixture to 100 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Filter the cooled infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix the filtered infusion with coconut oil at a ratio of 1:2 and beeswax at a ratio of 1:0.5, and heat the mixture to 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.
Pour the warm cream into sterile jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for 48 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with gas in stomach because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, a key compound in turmeric, has been shown to soothe digestive issues by reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles in the stomach and intestines. This can help alleviate symptoms of bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with gas in the stomach. Additionally, turmeric's carminative properties help to expel trapped gas from the body, promoting a sense of relief and comfort for those suffering from digestive distress.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric creams contain curcuminoids, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of gas and bloating.

Carminative properties: Turmeric has carminative properties, which means it helps to prevent the formation of gas bubbles in the stomach and intestines, reducing the discomfort caused by belching, bloating, and flatulence.

Antispasmodic properties: Turmeric creams also contain antispasmodic compounds that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to gas and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Turmeric are rich in curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe stomach discomfort and reduce inflammation associated with gas.
Roots: Turmeric roots contain a high amount of volatile oils, which are responsible for their antispasmodic properties, helping to relieve stomach cramps and spasms caused by gas.
remedy_recipe: Collect and dry high-quality turmeric roots and grind them into fine powder measuring 10 grams per serving.
Mix the ground turmeric powder with sweet almond oil and beeswax pellets in a ratio of 3:1:1, totaling 30 grams.
Add vitamin e oil and camphor essential oil to the mixture at dosages of 5 milliliters and 0.5 milliliters respectively, stirring well after each addition.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10 minutes or until it reaches a temperature of 60 degrees celsius.
Pour the warm mixture into glass containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with gas in stomach because its soothing properties can calm down digestive issues, reducing bloating and discomfort. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds work to ease muscle spasms in the digestive tract, allowing for a smoother passage of gas and alleviating symptoms such as cramps, nausea, and diarrhea. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxant properties can help regulate bowel movements, further relieving gas-related discomfort and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Chamomile cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to gas and bloating.
Antispasmodic effects: The antispasmodic properties of chamomile calm muscle spasms in the digestive system, reducing cramps and discomfort associated with gas and bloating.

Soothing astringent properties: Chamomile's soothing astringent properties help tighten the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing gas and bloating by preventing excessive fermentation and gas buildup.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers are a popular choice for making medicinal creams due to their calming and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe stomach discomfort and reduce bloating.
Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that has been shown to relax muscle contractions in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of gas and indigestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh chamomilla flowers and dry them completely using shade or low-temperature drying methods.
Grind 100g of dried chamomilla flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 20% w/w of the powdered chamomilla with 80% w/w of petroleum jelly in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture every 10 minutes to ensure even heating and incorporation until it reaches a temperature of 60°c to 70°c.
Allow the cream to cool to room temperature before transferring it into a sterilized container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain glycyrrhizin, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. Flavonoids: These plant compounds have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the gut, which can contribute to reduced gas and bloating.
Triterpenes: Specifically, triterpenoids like glycyrrhizic acid have been shown to inhibit the production of gas-producing enzymes in the gut, reducing symptoms of bloating and flatulence.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain a compound called glycyrrhizin, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive issues, making them effective in treating gas in the stomach.

remedy_recipe: Measure 20 grams of dried licorice root powder into a mortar and pestle.
Add 10 milliliters of jojoba oil and mix until you get a smooth paste.
Combine the mixture with 5 grams of beeswax and heat gently for 10 minutes to melt.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain natural ingredients that stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of bloating. Seeds: Containing essential oils like carvone and limonene, seeds have a natural antispasmodic effect, relieving cramps and reducing bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh anethum graveolens leaves and dry them in shade for 3-4 days to preserve their properties.
Grind dried anethum graveolens leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle, yielding 20 grams of extract.
Combine 10 grams of the powdered anethum graveolens with 50 grams of sweet almond oil in a double boiler, stirring until mixture reaches 65°c.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 30 minutes at 65°c, then slowly heat it up to 75°c over 15 minutes while stirring constantly, maintaining this temperature for another 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids and allowing cream to cool before transferring it to an airtight jar for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Balm-of-gilead creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain compounds that soothe and calm irritated digestive tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Volatile oils: The volatile oils found in Balm of Gilead, such as boswellia and camphor, can help to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, thereby reducing symptoms of gas and bloating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is used due to its anti-inflammatory and digestive properties, which help soothe and calm the stomach lining to reduce gas and discomfort.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried populus balsamifera bark from a reliable source.
Mix the harvested bark with 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 hours under gentle heat and constant stirring to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the solids from the liquid.
Mix 100g of beeswax with 50ml of the strained oil and heat until melted to create a smooth cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain natural compounds that aid digestion and alleviate symptoms of bloating. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of salvia officinalis from your garden or purchase them from a reputable supplier measuring out 20g for use.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator on low heat setting for 6 hours until crumbly and brittle texture forms.
Grind dried salvia officinalis leaves into a fine powder using an electric coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes to ensure uniformity.
Combine ground salvia officinalis powder with 100ml of distilled water in a double boiler stirring occasionally until a smooth paste forms within 10 minutes preparation time.
Add 50g of beeswax pellets and stir gently until melted and fully incorporated into the paste within 15 minutes heating period.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with gas in stomach because they contain a unique combination of anti-inflammatory and soothing compounds. The cream's main ingredient, Marrubium vulgare, has been traditionally used to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the stomach. When applied topically to the abdomen, the cream can help ease discomfort and bloating caused by trapped gas, allowing for natural digestion and relief from symptoms associated with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that help to relax muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of bloating and discomfort.
Barks: The barks have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated tissues and reduce inflammation in the stomach, alleviating gas and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Collect one ounce of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for five minutes.
Mix the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 10 grams of beeswax, 5 grams of coconut oil, and 20 drops of lavender essential oil in a heat-proof glass bowl.
Place the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water and melt the beeswax and coconut oil for ten minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to around 100°f before whipping it with an electric mixer for five minutes until it thickens into a creamy consistency.
Pour the herbal ginkgo biloba cream into sterilized glass jars and store them in the refrigerator for up to six months, allowing it to solidify at room temperature for thirty minutes before use.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry malva sylvestris flowers in a well-ventilated area for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried malva sylvestris flowers with two parts sweet almond oil and coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, allowing the oils to infuse with the herbs' properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for three days before whipping it with a hand mixer to create a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Dry 50 grams of fresh syzygium aromaticum bark in a low-temperature oven at 30°c for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried syzygium aromaticum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of grated coconut oil with 5 grams of beeswax pellets and 20 grams of distilled water in a double boiler.
Add the syzygium aromaticum powder to the mixture in step 3 and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and allow it to cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before whipping into a smooth cream.

remedy_recipe: Grind dried illicium verum leaves into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 20 grams of the ground illicium verum with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to facilitate absorption.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding any solids, and let it cool completely at room temperature.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried angelica archangelica root powder 20 grams and coconut oil 100 milliliters.
Combine the powdered angelica archangelica root 15 grams with jojoba oil 50 millilitres in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to allow infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container and add beeswax 10 grams.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature for at least 2 hours before packaging.

remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of dried carum carvi seeds with 500ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 30 grams of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat until melted and smooth.
Add the infused carum carvi liquid, 2 teaspoons of glycerin, and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil to the melted wax and oil mixture, stirring well.
Pour the combined mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_parts: Roots: Cardamom roots have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including flatulence, due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizome of cardamom contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol, which help to soothe the stomach and reduce gas formation.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 teaspoons of dried elettaria cardamomum pods into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground cardamom powder with 4 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and the mixture reaches 120°f to 130°f.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping the mixture with a hand mixer or whisk for 5 minutes until it thickens.
Pour the cooled cream into a sterilized jar and store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or freeze for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek creams helps with gas in stomach because it contains compounds like mucilage, flavonoids, and saponins that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms of digestive discomfort. The mucilage helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the stomach, reducing inflammation and irritation that can lead to bloating and flatulence. Meanwhile, the flavonoids and saponins enhance digestion and absorption of nutrients, promoting a balanced gut microbiome and regular bowel movements. This natural combination provides effective relief from gas and discomfort in the stomach.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Fenugreek cream's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to gas and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of fenugreek cream help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms such as bloating, cramping, and flatulence.
Prebiotic property: Fenugreek's prebiotic fiber helps to feed good bacteria in the gut, promoting a healthy balance of gut flora and reducing symptoms of gas, bloating, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gas.
Seeds: Contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of gas and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Measure two tablespoons of dried fenugreek leaves and crush them into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine one cup of distilled water with one tablespoon of honey and heat it over low flame for five minutes.
Add the crushed fenugreek powder to the warm liquid mixture and stir well to form a smooth paste.
Pour the paste into sterilized glass jars and store them in refrigerator for at least four hours or overnight.
After preparation time has elapsed, remove the creams from fridge and use as needed applying two to three times daily on stomach area with gentle massage.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gas in stomach are a natural and effective way to alleviate discomfort caused by excessive gas and bloating. These creams typically contain soothing herbs like peppermint, chamomile, and ginger, which help to calm digestive issues and reduce inflammation. For example, products like Gas-X or Beano contain herbs that aid digestion and prevent the buildup of gas in the stomach, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and confident. By using these creams, people can enjoy a sense of relief from persistent bloating and discomfort, improving their overall quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gas in stomach with herbal creams is to apply a soothing balm containing peppermint oil, chamomile extract, or ginger essential oil directly to the abdomen. These natural ingredients help ease digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate discomfort caused by gas. Look for creams with these ingredients specifically labeled as "gas relief" or "digestive aid" products. Regular application can provide effective relief from stomach gas and discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gas in stomach that are treated with herbal creams are indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Herbal creams containing ingredients like peppermint oil, chamomile, and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues. These creams can provide relief from cramps, discomfort, and inflammation associated with these conditions, offering an alternative to conventional treatments.
